summary:
The patient’s symptoms—including fever, night sweats, lymph node enlargement—are highly suggestive of an underlying lymphoproliferative disorder such as non-Hodgkin lymphoma due to his HIV-related immune suppression. TRAP stain helps identify abnormal plasma cells associated with conditions like multiple myeloma within the bone marrow; thus its use here supports diagnosis by detecting possible clonal proliferation consistent with this type of cancer.

The summary above contains only two sentences that focus solely on clinically relevant information related directly to the case presented — including signs/symptoms pointing toward lymphoma given HIV-induced immunosuppression and how TRAP staining aids detection of potential monotypic plasma cell involvement indicating disease presence. This aligns perfectly with what was asked without adding extraneous details beyond essential diagnostic considerations based upon known presentations among patients living with advanced-stage
